heading: Imposition of sanctions with respect to the transfer of goods or technologies to Iran that are likely to be used to commit human rights abuses
label: 8514a Imposition of sanctions with respect to the transfer of goods or technologies to Iran that are likely to be used to commit human rights abuses

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The President shall impose sanctions in accordance with subsection (c) with respect to each person on the list required by subsection (b).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the President shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees a list of persons that the President determines have knowingly engaged in an activity described in paragraph (2) on or after .August 10, 2012August 10, 2012

children.label: i
children.text: transfers, or facilitates the transfer of, goods or technologies described in subparagraph (C) to Iran, any entity organized under the laws of Iran or otherwise subject to the jurisdiction of the Government of Iran, or any national of Iran, for use in or with respect to Iran; or

children.label: ii
children.text: provides services (including services relating to hardware, software, and specialized information, and professional consulting, engineering, and support services) with respect to goods or technologies described in subparagraph (C) after such goods or technologies are transferred to Iran.

children.heading: Applicability to contracts and other agreements
children.label: B Applicability to contracts and other agreements
children.text: A person engages in an activity described in subparagraph (A) without regard to whether the activity is carried out pursuant to a contract or other agreement entered into before, on, or after .August 10, 2012

children.label: i
children.text: firearms or ammunition (as those terms are defined in ), rubber bullets, police batons, pepper or chemical sprays, stun grenades, electroshock weapons, tear gas, water cannons, or surveillance technology; orsection 921 of title 18

children.label: ii
children.text: sensitive technology (as defined in ).section 8515(c) of this title

children.heading: Special rule to allow for termination of sanctionable activity
children.label: 3 Special rule to allow for termination of sanctionable activity
A
children.text: the person is no longer engaging in, or has taken significant verifiable steps toward stopping, the activity described in paragraph (2) for which the President would otherwise have included the person on the list; and

children.label: B
children.text: the President has received reliable assurances that the person will not knowingly engage in any activity described in paragraph (2) in the future.

children.label: A
children.text: each time the President is required to submit an updated list to those committees under ; andsection 8514(b)(2)(A) of this title

children.heading: Form
children.label: A Form
children.text: The list required by paragraph (1) shall be submitted in unclassified form but may contain a classified annex.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: B Public availability
children.text: The unclassified portion of the list required by paragraph (1) shall be made available to the public and posted on the websites of the Department of the Treasury and the Department of State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the President shall impose sanctions described in with respect to a person on the list required by subsection (b).section 8514(c) of this title

children.heading: Transfers to Iran’s Revolutionary Guard Corps
children.label: 2 Transfers to Iran’s Revolutionary Guard Corps
A
children.text: impose sanctions described in with respect to the person; andsection 8514(c) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: impose such other sanctions from among the sanctions described in section 6(a) of the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996 (; note) as the President determines appropriate.Public Law 104–17250 U.S.C. 1701
